summary.intent: Implement Matrix OIDC authentication (Issue #711) to support homeservers using Matrix Authentication Service (MAS/MSC3861), since password auth is increasingly rare and blocking users like those on matrix.org since April 2025.
summary.outcome: Full OIDC auth flow implemented end-to-end across Rust backend and JS frontend, merged to PR #730. Login popup works, channels appear connected, but moltis_owned cross-signing bootstrap requires MAS browser approval and the UI update after approval requires a page reload.

repo: ChannelPlugin trait methods must be in `impl ChannelPlugin for MatrixPlugin`, not inherent `impl MatrixPlugin` — gateway dispatches via `dyn ChannelPlugin` so only trait methods are visible
RPC responses use `res.payload` not `res.result` for data fields
MAS (matrix.org) rejects `https://localhost` redirect URIs per RFC 8252 — must use `http://localhost`
MAS rejects localhost `client_uri` — must be a real HTTPS project URL (e.g. docs.moltis.org)
`client.matrix_auth().logged_in()` returns false for OIDC sessions; use `client.user_id().is_some()` for auth-agnostic connected check
`bootstrap_cross_signing_if_needed(None)` is a no-op when cross-signing already exists from a prior session — must call `reset_identity()` instead when device lacks signing keys
MAS requires explicit browser approval at `https://account.matrix.org/account/?action=org.matrix.cross_signing_reset` for each new device cross-signing reset
ChannelEvent serialization uses `#[serde(tag = "kind", rename_all = "snake_case")]` — new variants auto-serialize to snake_case kind strings
Ownership config fields (ownership_mode, dm_policy, etc.) were not forwarded through the oauth_start RPC — gateway was building a hardcoded minimal config

code.finding: impl ChannelPlugin for MatrixPlugin ends here — OIDC methods must be added inside this block, not as inherent methods on MatrixPlugin
Original ownership check used `matrix_auth().logged_in()` which always returns false for OIDC sessions
Ownership result stored here in sync_once_and_spawn_loop — StatusChanged event must be emitted after this line
ownershipIssue detection requires ownershipError.length > 0 — if status poll fires before ownership bootstrap completes, ownershipError is empty and approval UI never shows
Added StatusChanged event variant — JS handleChannelEvent should call loadChannels() on 'status_changed' kind to refresh ownership card without page reload
provider-btn has `transition: opacity 0.15s` — combined with disabled state change, causes Safari to composite old painted text pixels over new text (ghost button bug)

workflow: Never ship OAuth redirect URI logic without testing against the real identity provider — MAS has specific requirements (http loopback, real client_uri) not catchable with unit tests

Safari ghost text on buttons: Preact patching text nodes inside `<button>` doesn't always trigger repaint — fix by adding `key=` tied to the state that changes the text, forcing full DOM element recreation
3-second polling delays are fragile timing hacks — use WebSocket events (ChannelEvent::StatusChanged) to push state changes to the UI instead
When a Rust trait method isn't being called, check if it's an inherent method vs trait impl — only trait impl methods are callable through `dyn Trait`

Greptile code review is useful for catching security issues (token Debug exposure, blocking I/O in async, URL injection) but generates stale comments on old commit lines that pile up across iterations

friction: Three separate MAS registration errors requiring iterative fixes without a test MAS instance: invalid_redirect_uri (https vs http), invalid client_uri (localhost rejected), wrong callback path (/api/oauth/callback vs /auth/callback)
OIDC trait methods silently fell back to default noop — no compile error, no runtime panic, just 'OIDC login not supported' at runtime
Multiple light-text-on-light-background color contrast bugs across emerald, sky, amber, and blue banners requiring individual fixes
Ghost button rendering in Safari required 5+ fix attempts before finding that keying on state string forces DOM recreation
Ownership bootstrap timing race: JS poll detects 'connected' before sync_once_and_spawn_loop finishes ownership checks, so UI closes modal without showing approval state
Each new OIDC device triggers a fresh MAS browser approval requirement for cross-signing reset, making moltis_owned mode cumbersome to test
Channel list requires page reload to show ownership approval UI — WebSocket event fix was implemented but not confirmed working at transcript end
Greptile accumulated stale comments across 4 review iterations as it re-flagged already-fixed issues on new commit lines
open_items: OIDC channels not auto-started from store on Moltis restart — OIDC session restore from disk is unimplemented, channels show as disconnected after restart
pending_identity_reset handle is in-memory only — lost on restart, causing 'unknown channel account' error on retry ownership after restart
WebSocket StatusChanged event emission added but real-time UI update without page reload not confirmed working at end of session
Recovery always shows 'disabled' after OIDC ownership bootstrap — recovery setup (secret storage) not implemented for OIDC path
m.secret_storage.default_key deserialization warning on every sync — upstream matrix-sdk issue with malformed secret storage from prior sessions
E2E Playwright tests mock channels.oauth_start but don't test against real MAS — full OIDC flow integration tests not possible without test MAS instance
Duplicate account guard added for oidc_complete but same Matrix user connected via different auth modes (password + OIDC) is not prevented
Re-authenticate button in EditMatrixModal mentioned in plan but not implemented — OIDC channels have no way to refresh tokens without removing and re-adding
